noi_lai_luhang_2009.gold: A graduate of Hangzhou No. 2 High School, Lai won a gold medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics, ranking fourth. In 2010, he earned a gold medal at the 22nd International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI), placing 11th globally. He was subsequently admitted to Peking University’s School of Electronics Engineering and Computer Science.

noi_li_xiaoxiao_2009.gold: Li Xiaoxiao is the co-founder and algorithm lead of Beijing Zhengding Private Equity. A 2009 NOI gold medalist and national training team member from Fuzhou No. 1 High School, he graduated from Tsinghua University and earned a PhD in artificial intelligence from the Chinese University of Hong Kong under SenseTime founder Tang Xiaoou.

noi_li_xinye_2009.gold: Graduating from Tsinghua University’s "Yao Class," Li ranked second nationally in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics. He is the founder and CEO of Alpha Star Research, a New York-based quantitative trading firm. An influential online commentator, he is known for his controversial social essays and public disputes with his father, developer Li Songjian.

noi_wu_jiajun_2009.gold: Wu Jiajun is an Assistant Professor of Computer Science at Stanford University. A 2009 NOI gold medalist from East China Normal University Second High School, he graduated from Tsinghua University’s Yao Class and received the 2013 Special Scholarship. He earned his PhD from MIT and is a prominent researcher in computer vision, machine learning, and robotics.

noi_wu_yi_2009.gold: Wu Yi is a computer scientist and 2009 NOI gold medalist from Changzhou Senior High School. An IOI 2010 silver medalist, he graduated from Tsinghua’s Yao Class and earned a PhD from UC Berkeley. A former OpenAI researcher and NIPS 2016 Best Paper winner, he is a Tsinghua University assistant professor specializing in reinforcement learning.

noi_mo_tao_2009.gold: Mo Tao is a competitive programmer from Changsha Changjun High School who ranked first at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics. He is the inventor of Mo's algorithm (莫队算法), a square root decomposition technique for offline range queries. He attended Tsinghua University and was a member of the 2010 Chinese National Training Team.

noi_pan_yuchao_2009.gold: Pan Yuchao is a competitive programmer from Shaoxing No. 1 Middle School. He won a gold medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a silver medal at the 2010 International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI). He joined Tsinghua University’s Yao Class and won gold at the 2009 APIO and 2010 CTSC.

noi_chen_keqing_2009.gold: Born in 1991, Chen Keqing is a Tsinghua University alumnus and the fifth employee of Megvii (旷视科技), where he led the development of facial recognition systems for major Android brands. In 2020, he founded Jaha Technology (极豪科技), specializing in AI-driven fingerprint sensors. He also chaired the Tsinghua Computer Science Department's student science association.

noi_chen_gaoyuan_2009.gold: A competitive programmer who won a gold medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He graduated from Tsinghua University and earned a master's degree from the University of Southern California. Known by the handle cgy4ever, he achieved Topcoder Target status and has served as a prominent problem setter for Topcoder.

noi_li_yuliang_2009.gold: Following his 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics gold medal, Li Yuliang was admitted to Tsinghua University. There, he won a third-place ACM regional award and published six research papers, including one as first author. He later pursued a PhD in Computer Science at the University of Southern California.

noi_lin_yankai_2009.gold: Lin Yankai is an associate professor at Renmin University of China’s Gaoling School of Artificial Intelligence. A 2009 NOI gold medalist from Shantou Jinshan Middle School, he earned his PhD from Tsinghua University in 2019. He previously worked as a senior researcher at Tencent WeChat AI and currently serves on the CCF NOI Scientific Committee.

noi_wang_yining_2009.gold: Wang is an Associate Professor of Operations Management at UT Dallas. A Tsinghua University Yao Class alumnus, he earned a PhD in Machine Learning from Carnegie Mellon University. He was a 2010 IOI China National Training Team member. His research on sequential decision-making and revenue management appears in journals like Management Science and Operations Research.

noi_lyu_weicong_2009.gold: Lyu was a member of the Chinese national training team and was admitted to Peking University’s Yuanpei College through recommendation. While at Nanjing Foreign Language School, he served as a class monitor and earned a recommendation to Fudan University during his first year of high school after scoring 390 out of 400 in the provincial informatics league.

noi_song_wenjie_2009.gold: A 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) gold medalist from Suzhou High School, Song Wenjie ranked 14th nationally and was selected for the IOI 2010 China National Training Team. He was subsequently admitted to Tsinghua University. He is recognized for his contributions to the book *Node.js Development Guide* authored by Guo Jiabao.

noi_li_kaiwei_2009.gold: Li Kaiwei attended Tsinghua University’s Department of Computer Science and Technology, where he later pursued a master's degree in high-performance computing. In 2015, he served as the coach for the Tsinghua supercomputing team, leading them to a "Grand Slam" by winning the ASC, ISC, and SC international student supercomputing competitions.

noi_zhang_fang_2009.gold: Zhang Fang is a quantum computing researcher who placed first at the 2011 China Team Selection Contest. A graduate of Tsinghua University’s Yao Class and the University of Michigan, he previously served as a quantum scientist at Alibaba and currently works at Zhongguancun Laboratory, specializing in quantum error correction and fault-tolerant computing.

noi_chen_jianfei_2009.gold: Chen Jianfei is a tenure-track associate professor at Tsinghua University’s Department of Computer Science and Technology. A 2009 NOI gold medalist from Shandong Normal University Affiliated High School, he earned his PhD from Tsinghua in 2019. He specializes in efficient machine learning and developed open-source systems including ActNN, WarpLDA, and the ZhuSuan probabilistic programming library.

noi_he_pufan_2009.gold: He Pufan is a two-time gold medalist in the China National Olympiad in Informatics, winning in 2009 and 2010. He graduated from the Institute for Interdisciplinary Information Sciences (Yao Class) at Tsinghua University and subsequently became a software engineer at Google.

noi_s_zhou_yisong_2009.gold: Zhou Yisong is a competitive programmer who won a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics. He attended Peking University, where he earned gold medals at the 2011 ACM-ICPC Asia Regional Contests in Dalian and Shanghai. He later pursued graduate studies in computational mathematics at Peking University and co-authored research on smoothed particle hydrodynamics algorithms.

noi_s_fan_haoqiang_2009.gold: Fan Haoqiang is a computer scientist and entrepreneur. A 2011 International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI) gold medalist, he graduated from Tsinghua University’s Yao Class. He was the sixth employee and a research director at Megvii (Face++), leading key computer vision developments. He later co-founded the embodied AI startup Dexmal (原力灵机).

noi_feng_qiwei_2009.gold: Feng Qiwei is a competitive programmer from Zhongshan No. 1 Middle School. He won gold medals at the 2009 and 2010 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). Representing China at the 2010 International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI), he earned a gold medal, ranking tenth globally and first on the Chinese team. He attended Tsinghua University.

noi_s_wang_zhengyu_2009.gold: Wang Zhengyu is a competitive programmer from Northeast Yucai School. He won a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and earned first prizes in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) in 2007 and 2008. In 2010, he was admitted to Tsinghua University as a recommended student.

noi_guo_jiabao_2009.gold: Guo Jiabao, known as BYVoid, is a software engineer and open-source developer. A Tsinghua University alumnus, he authored the *Node.js Development Guide* and created OpenCC, a widely used library for Simplified and Traditional Chinese conversion. He has held engineering roles at Google in Japan and Switzerland and is a prominent "digital nomad."

noi_s_liu_yining_2009.gold: Liu Yining is a competitive programmer from Changsha Yali Middle School. He won a silver medal at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and earned a first prize in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP). In 2010, he was admitted to Tsinghua University as a recommended student.

noi_s_ni_jinan_2009.gold: Ni Jinan won silver medals at the 2009 and 2010 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) representing Fujian Normal University Affiliated High School. He was admitted to Peking University in 2011, where he earned a Master’s degree in Computer Software and Theory and co-invented a patent for multi-source heterogeneous data collection in software projects.

noi_s_wu_shang_2009.gold: Wu Shang is an informatics competitor from Nanjing Foreign Language School. He won the first-place silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a gold medal at NOI 2010. He also earned a gold medal at the 2010 Asia-Pacific Informatics Olympiad and was admitted to Peking University as a high school freshman.

noi_s_fan_shunhao_2009.gold: Fan Shunhao is a computer scientist and entrepreneur. A two-time NOI silver medalist and 2010 APIO gold medalist, he graduated from Tsinghua University’s Yao Class, serving as its first student council chairman. He founded Hongyu Technology (IRISVIEW), where he developed the IRIS OS, a 3D operating system for spatial computing and XR devices.

noi_s_deng_yongxing_2009.gold: Deng Yongxing is a competitive programmer from East China Normal University Affiliated High School. He earned silver medals at the National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) in 2009 and 2010, and the 2010 Asia-Pacific Informatics Olympiad. He was admitted to Stanford University in 2011, where he co-authored the machine learning report "The Recipe Learner."

noi_s_lyu_xiao_2009.gold: Lyu Xiao is a competitive programmer from Shandong Normal University Affiliated High School. He won first prizes in the 2007 and 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) before earning a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He was subsequently admitted to Peking University through the recommendation process.

noi_s_wei_ming_2009.gold: Wei Ming earned a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a gold medal (third place) at NOI 2010 representing The High School Affiliated to Shandong Normal University. A Tsinghua University graduate, he won an ACM-ICPC regional third-place award and joined Megvii (Face++), specializing in performance optimization for heterogeneous platforms.

noi_s_pan_zheyi_2009.gold: A competitive programmer from Fujian Normal University Affiliated High School, Pan earned silver medals at the National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) in both 2009 and 2010. He also received a domestic gold medal at the 2010 Asia-Pacific Informatics Olympiad (APIO). In 2011, he was admitted to the ACM Class at Shanghai Jiao Tong University.

noi_s_fu_qiang_2009.gold: Fu Qiang won a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), ranking 28th. A student at Shandong Experimental High School (Class of 2007), he secured an on-site recruitment agreement with Peking University following his achievement. He also earned a first-prize award in the 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP).

noi_s_shu_ruiwen_2009.gold: Shu Ruiwen is a mathematician and Assistant Professor at the University of Georgia. A 2009 NOI silver medalist from RDFZ, he earned his bachelor’s from Peking University and a PhD from the University of Wisconsin-Madison. He received the 2026 Peter Lax Award for his research on hyperbolic conservation laws and collective dynamics.

noi_s_sun_xiangyan_2009.gold: Sun Xiangyan is a computer scientist and entrepreneur. A 2009 NOI silver medalist from Hefei No. 1 High School, he graduated from Fudan University and won the 2014 Microsoft "Beauty of Programming" Challenge. He co-founded Accutar Biotechnology, serving as CTO and leading AI-driven drug discovery through deep learning models like Chemi-Net.

noi_s_yan_songbai_2009.gold: Yan Songbai won a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) representing Chongqing No. 1 Middle School. He graduated from Peking University’s School of Electronics Engineering and Computer Science, where he was a member of the Computing Introduction Experimental Class. He later pursued a PhD at the University of California, San Diego.

noi_s_liu_yukun_2009.gold: Liu Yukun is a competitive programmer from Baoding No. 2 Middle School in Hebei Province. He earned a silver medal (second prize) at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009) with a score of 330. He also achieved a first-prize ranking in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) for Hebei.

noi_s_xiang_shang_2009.gold: Xiang Shang is a competitive programmer from Changsha Yali Middle School. He won a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a gold medal at the 2010 China Team Selection Contest (CTSC). Admitted to Peking University in 2011, he was also featured on the variety show Day Day Up in 2009.

noi_s_yang_huan_2009.gold: Yang Huan is a competitive programmer from Tianjin Nankai High School. He won a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a bronze medal at the 2008 NOI. He also earned a second prize in the 2009 National High School Mathematics League within the Tianjin competition zone.

noi_s_jiang_linhao_2009.gold: Admitted to Tsinghua University as a recommended student, Jiang was the class monitor of the renowned 2010 "most powerful class" at No. 2 High School of East China Normal University. A 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics silver medalist, he led student-run informatics training and is a peer of computer scientist Wu Jiajun.

noi_s_hu_zhangguangda_2009.gold: Hu Zhangguangda is a competitive programmer from Fuzhou No. 1 High School. He earned a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). While studying in the ACM Class at Shanghai Jiao Tong University, he won a gold medal and the Asian championship at the 2012 ACM-ICPC World Finals, placing fourth globally.

noi_s_huang_cai_2009.gold: A 2009 NOI silver medalist from Baoding No. 2 Middle School, Huang Cai studied in the ACM Class at Shanghai Jiao Tong University. He won the 2012 Microsoft "Beauty of Programming" National Challenge and a 2010 ACM-ICPC Asia gold medal. He later co-founded Fangti Chinese and served as VP of Product at SmartStudy.

noi_s_huang_yifeng_2009.gold: Huang Yifeng is the co-founder and CEO of Hypergryph, the developer of the mobile game *Arknights*. A graduate of Shanghai Jiao Tong University, he previously worked as a software engineer at Google. He was recognized on the 2025 Hurun U35 China Entrepreneur Pioneers list and served as the technical lead for *Arknights* during its development.

noi_s_zhou_yichao_2009.gold: Zhou Yichao is a computer scientist and research scientist at Apple. An alumnus of Fudan University Affiliated High School, he won a silver medal at the 2009 NOI and represented China at the 2011 IOI, winning silver. He holds a B.Eng. from Tsinghua’s Yao Class and a PhD in computer science from UC Berkeley.

noi_s_wu_hao_2009.gold: Wu Hao (吴豪) is a competitive programmer from Wuhan Iron and Steel No. 3 High School. He won a silver medal at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He contributed to the informatics community by authoring and translating technical articles on algorithms, such as tree arrays and 0-1 fractional programming.

noi_s_bai_yanbo_2009.gold: Bai Yanbo is a 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) silver medalist from Xi'an No. 83 Middle School. He was admitted to Fudan University's School of Computer Science in 2010. He gained public attention for marrying classmate Wei Chuchu upon their 2014 graduation, following a high-profile confession during their freshman military training.

noi_s_ren_chunxu_2009.gold: Ren Chunxu is a competitive programmer who studied at Shanghai Jiao Tong University. A member of the SJTU ACM-ICPC team, he won a gold medal at the 2011 ACM-ICPC Beijing Regional. He contributed to the book *ACM International Collegiate Programming Contest: Algorithms and Implementation* and later conducted game artificial intelligence research at NetEase.

noi_s_ma_hao_2009.gold: Ma Hao is a competitive programmer from Henan Experimental High School. He earned a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and the 2009 Asia-Pacific Informatics Olympiad (APIO). In 2010, he was admitted to Fudan University through the national recommendation program for distinguished competition winners.

noi_s_wang_mo_2009.gold: Wang Mo is a competitive programmer from Wuhan Iron and Steel No. 3 High School. He won a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) with a score of 280. Previously, he earned a first prize in the 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) while representing the same institution.

noi_s_qian_qiao_2009.gold: Qian Qiao (钱桥) is a competitive programmer from Tianjin Nankai High School. He won silver medals at the China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) in both 2009 and 2010. He also earned a first prize in the 2009 National High School Mathematics League (Tianjin region) and was a participant in the 2011 NOI Winter Camp.

noi_s_liu_ke_2009.gold: Liu Ke (刘坷) is a competitive programmer from Hunan Normal University High School. He won a silver medal at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009) and earned first prizes in the 2008 and 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) for Hunan. He was coached by Li Shuping.

noi_b_cheng_longju_2009.gold: A graduate of Chongqing Luneng Bashu Secondary School, Cheng won a first prize in the 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) before earning a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). In 2010, he was admitted to the Science Experimental Class at Renmin University of China via the recommendation process.

noi_s_li_guanru_2009.gold: Li Guanru (李冠儒) is a competitive programmer from Tianjin Nankai High School. Representing Tianjin, he won a silver medal at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009). He also achieved first-prize rankings in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) in 2009 and 2010, having previously attended Nankai Xiangyu School.

noi_s_li_peiqian_2009.gold: Li Peiqian is a competitive programmer who won a bronze medal at the 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He earned first prizes in the 2006 and 2009 NOIP. He also developed an "Intelligent Wheelchair" project, which received an award at the 20th Jilin Youth Science and Technology Innovation Contest in the engineering category.

noi_b_li_bofang_2009.gold: A competitive programmer from Sichuan, Li Bofang represented Mianyang Nanshan High School at the National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), earning bronze medals in 2008 and 2009. In 2010, he received recommended admission to Tsinghua University, where he studied Nuclear Engineering and Nuclear Technology under a China National Nuclear Corporation targeted program.

noi_b_zhang_xue_2009.gold: Zhang Xue is a competitive programmer from Mianyang Nanshan High School in Sichuan. At the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), she earned a bronze medal and was honored as the Best Female Contestant. She also achieved a first-prize ranking in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) Sichuan division.

noi_s_xiang_ziqing_2009.gold: Xiang Ziqing is an associate professor at SUSTech. A 2009 NOI and 2010 CMO silver medalist from Harbin Normal University Affiliated High School, he earned a B.Sc. from Shanghai Jiao Tong University and a Ph.D. from the University of Georgia. He received the 2022 Kirkman Medal for contributions to design theory and algebraic combinatorics.

noi_b_miao_peihan_2009.gold: Miao Peihan is the John E. Savage Assistant Professor of Computer Science at Brown University. An alumna of the Shanghai Jiao Tong University ACM Honors Class and UC Berkeley, she won the Best Female Contestant award at NOI 2009. Her cryptography research has received NSF CAREER, Google, and Amazon research awards.

noi_b_xie_luyu_2009.gold: Xie Luyu is a competitive programmer from Fuzhou No.1 High School. He earned a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a first prize in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP). In 2010, he was admitted to Fudan University to study computer science and technology as a recommended student.

noi_b_wang_enze_2009.gold: Wang Enze is a competitive programmer from Inner Mongolia. As a Grade 11 student at Baotou No. 9 Middle School, he won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) with a score of 250. He also achieved a first prize in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP).

noi_b_ye_dan_2009.gold: Ye Dan is a competitive programmer from Chongqing Bashu Secondary School. She won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a bronze medal at the 2009 Asia-Pacific Informatics Olympiad (APIO) China region. She also received a first prize in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP).

noi_b_fan_rundong_2009.gold: Fan Rundong is a co-founder of Hypergryph and the lead designer of the mobile game *Arknights*. A software engineering alumnus of Shanghai Jiao Tong University (BS 2014, MS 2016), he was honored as an Outstanding Alumnus in 2023. He is also known by the nickname "Donggua" and was a 2009 NOI bronze medalist.

noi_b_guo_xiaoxu_2009.gold: Guo Xiaoxu is a competitive programmer known by the handle "ftiasch" and nickname "Chajie." Representing Shanghai Jiao Tong University, he earned gold medals at the 2012 and 2013 ACM-ICPC World Finals, finishing as the global runner-up in 2013. He founded ICPC Camp and won a second bronze medal at the 2010 National Olympiad in Informatics.

noi_b_li_yi_2009.gold: Li Yi (李艺) is a competitive programmer from Shandong, China. Representing the High School Attached to Shandong Normal University, he won a bronze medal (Third Prize) at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). At the time of the competition in July 2009, he was a tenth-grade student.

noi_b_liang_jingyuan_2009.gold: Liang Jingyuan (梁景元) is a competitive programmer from Guangxi, China. Representing the Guangxi Normal University Affiliated Foreign Language School, he won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He also earned a first prize in the 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) senior group.

noi_b_li_bojie_2009.gold: A computer scientist and entrepreneur, Li graduated from the USTC Special Class for the Gifted Young and earned a joint PhD from USTC and Microsoft Research Asia. He was among the first Huawei "Genius Youth" recruits, serving as a deputy chief expert. He co-founded Pine AI and created the widely used USTC Course Community.

noi_b_xu_ranci_2009.gold: Xu Ranci is an informatics competition coach at Jiaxiang Innovation School. He earned bachelor’s and master’s degrees in computer science from Fudan University. Previously a senior engineer for ByteDance’s Douyin team, he specializes in AI and large language models. He twice qualified for the Sichuan provincial informatics team and was admitted to Fudan via recommendation.

noi_b_zhu_bingqi_2009.gold: Zhu Bingqi (朱炳琪) is a 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) bronze medalist from Hengshui High School in Hebei. She also won a first prize in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP). In 2010, she was recommended for admission to Fudan University’s School of Information Science and Engineering, later transferring to the Department of Physics.

noi_b_lyu_rundong_2009.gold: Lyu Rundong is a student from Chongqing No. 8 Secondary School who won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). In 2010, he was recommended for admission to Tsinghua University. He also won a second prize in the 2007 "Star of Outlook" English Talent Competition in the Chongqing region.

noi_b_zou_xun_2009.gold: A competitive programmer from Anhui, Zou Xun represented Bengbu No. 2 Middle School. He ranked second in Anhui in the 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) with 330 points. He won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics and received the 2010 Bengbu Mayor's Innovation Award (Best Nomination).

noi_b_zhang_chong_2009.gold: Zhang Chong represented Shaanxi Normal University High School in competitive programming, winning a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). In 2010, he was admitted to Zhejiang University as a recommended student (保送生) in recognition of his first-prize achievement in the national informatics league.

noi_b_zhong_yin_2009.gold: Zhong Yin is a competitive programmer from Daqing Experimental High School. He won a bronze medal at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) as an eleventh-grade student. He earned first-prize honors in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) in 2007 and 2008, and was qualified for university recommendation in 2010.

noi_b_rao_guoteng_2009.gold: A graduate of the Shanghai Jiao Tong University ACM Class, Rao won a third prize in the 2009 NOI team competition representing Sichuan. In 2011, he earned a silver medal at the ACM-ICPC Shanghai Regional with team Propeller. He also secured a first prize in the 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP).

noi_b_cai_ning_2009.gold: Cai Ning is a competitive programmer and mathematician from Jiangxi Province. Representing Jiujiang No. 1 Middle School, he won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). That same year, he also earned a first prize in the Jiangxi region of the National High School Mathematics League.

noi_b_alafate_2009.gold: Alafate (阿拉法特) is a computer scientist from Urumqi No. 1 High School. He won bronze medals at the 2007 and 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics. A Fudan University alumnus, he earned a PhD from UC San Diego and served as a Staff Research Scientist at Meta before founding a startup in San Francisco.

noi_b_chen_yi_2009.gold: Chen Yi (陈怡) is a Chinese competitive programmer who won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) representing Chongqing No. 8 Secondary School. She also earned a first prize in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) and later served as an informatics coach at the school's Hongfan campus.

noi_b_he_boshuo_2009.gold: He Boshuo is a graduate of the High School Affiliated to Renmin University of China. A 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) bronze medalist, he also received a prize at the inaugural Shing-Tung Yau High School Mathematics Awards. He attended Tsinghua University’s Institute for Interdisciplinary Information Sciences (Yao Class), winning the 2013 MCM/ICM Outstanding Winner award.

noi_b_yang_shubai_2009.gold: Yang Shubai is an assistant researcher at Tsinghua University’s Aero Engine Academy. A 2009 NOI bronze medalist from Urumqi No. 1 Senior High School, he earned his bachelor’s and PhD in aerospace propulsion from Beihang University. He specializes in aero-engine control systems and performance modeling, co-authoring the textbook *Aeroengine Control Principles* (2021).

noi_b_peng_yuhan_2009.gold: A competitive programmer from Wuhu No. 1 High School, Peng represented the Anhui provincial team at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics, earning a bronze medal. He previously achieved first-prize honors in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) in both 2007 and 2008, and was recognized as a 2010 Gaokao policy-favored candidate.

noi_b_li_runming_2009.gold: Li Runming is a two-time bronze medalist in the China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009, 2010) from Beijing No. 80 High School. He serves as Vice Chairman of the CCF Youth Education Subcommittee and a member of the CCF GESP Organizing Committee. He has held roles at Xin Xin Xiang Rong and Xueersi.

noi_b_yi_qian_2009.gold: Yi Qian is a competitive programmer from Changsha No. 1 High School who won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics. She was admitted to the ACM Class at Shanghai Jiao Tong University, where she earned silver medals and the Best Female Team award at the 2010 ACM-ICPC Asia Regional contests.

noi_b_sha_mo_2009.gold: Sha Mo is a 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) bronze medalist from Dalian Yuming Senior High School. Representing the Liaoning provincial team, he earned first-prize honors in the 2008 and 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP). In 2010, he was recommended for admission to the Harbin Institute of Technology.

noi_b_liang_jinhong_2009.gold: Liang Jinhong is a competitive programmer from Macau’s Hou Kong Middle School. He won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a gold medal at the 2008 Macau Olympiad in Informatics (MOI). He also earned first prize in the virtual robot soccer category at the 7th National IT Innovation and Practice Activities.

noi_b_ning_ke_2009.gold: Ning Ke is a competitive programmer from Jiujiang No. 1 Middle School. He won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a first prize in the 2009 NOIP. In 2011, he was admitted to Zhejiang University, where he later enrolled as a direct PhD student in computer science in 2015.

noi_b_hou_jiaxu_2009.gold: A competitive programmer from Zhengzhou 101 Middle School, Hou Jiaxu won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a first prize in the 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP). In 2010, he was admitted to Northwest Polytechnical University through the national recommendation program for talented students.

noi_b_xu_lunbo_2009.gold: Xu Lunbo won an Honorable Mention at the 2008 S.-T. Yau High School Mathematics Awards for research on regular polygons. A Peking University computer science graduate, he received the Best Poster Award at the 2013 Undergraduate Research Exhibition and won second prize in the 2012 Microsoft Student Challenge for developing a Kinect-based robot fish.

noi_b_dong_yuqian_2009.gold: A 2009 NOI bronze medalist, Dong Yuqian was admitted to Tsinghua University’s Yao Class in 2010. She earned a Master’s degree from the Institute for Interdisciplinary Information Sciences (IIIS), where she conducted research in network science as a member of Wei Xu’s group and represented the university in the ACM-ICPC.

noi_b_wei_zhenggong_2009.gold: Wei Zhenggong was admitted to Fudan University in 2010 as a recommended student after winning a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) for Tangshan No. 1 High School. He later joined Shanghai Chiwu Information Technology (ChiefClouds) and was included in the 2023 Shanghai talent introduction list for his professional contributions.

noi_b_xing_jianan_2009.gold: Xing earned first-prize honors in the 2007, 2008, and 2009 NOIP. In 2009, he received the Beijing Municipal Silver Sail Award and a second prize in the Canadian Computing Competition (Beijing). He was admitted to Peking University in 2010 from Beijing No. 80 High School.

noi_b_jin_lingzi_2009.gold: Representing the Zhejiang provincial team, Jin Lingzi won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics. In 2010, she was admitted to Fudan University to major in Computer Science and Technology as a recommended student. She also earned a second prize in the 26th National Physics Olympiad (Zhejiang) under instructor Zhu Jinjun.

noi_b_tan_chuanqi_2009.gold: Tan Chuanqi is an algorithm expert at Alibaba DAMO Academy who earned his PhD from Beihang University through a joint program with Microsoft Research Asia. Since joining Alibaba in 2019, he has contributed to the AliceMind system and the Qwen (Tongyi Qianwen) series. He has won championships in MS-MARCO and TREC international evaluations.

noi_b_wang_sida_2009.gold: Wang Sida is a Chinese competitive programmer from Xi'an Tieyi High School. He won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a first prize in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP). In 2010, he was admitted to Xi'an Jiaotong University as a recommended student.

noi_b_liu_yingshan_2009.gold: Liu Yingshan is a competitive programmer from Sun Yat-sen Memorial Middle School. She won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). She also earned first-prize honors in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) in both 2009 and 2010, representing Guangdong Province in national informatics competitions.

noi_b_feng_dan_2009.gold: Feng Dan is a competitive programmer from East China Normal University Second High School. She won bronze medals at the National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) in 2009 and 2010. She also earned a bronze medal at the 2010 Asia-Pacific Informatics Olympiad (APIO) and was admitted to Tsinghua University in 2011 via recommendation.

noi_b_shen_haoquan_2009.gold: Shen Haoquan won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics representing Shanxi University Affiliated High School. He studied in the Qiushi Science Class at Zhejiang University and conducted research at Carnegie Mellon University. His work in computer vision and machine learning has been published in venues including CVPR, AAAI, and IEEE Transactions.

noi_b_zhao_zhiheng_2009.gold: Zhao Zhiheng is a competitive programmer and AI researcher. Representing Northeast Normal University Affiliated High School, he won bronze medals at the 2009 and 2010 National Olympiad in Informatics. He earned undergraduate and master's degrees from Tsinghua University’s School of Software and became a core developer for the Xingzhen Go (星阵围棋) artificial intelligence team.

noi_b_sun_su_2009.gold: A competitive programmer from Dalian Yuming Senior High School, Sun won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and was a member of the Liaoning Provincial Training Team. He previously earned a first prize in the 2008 NOIP and received honors in chemistry competitions. He later attended Georgia Tech.

noi_b_yan_jiabao_2009.gold: Yan Jiabao (闫家宝) is a competitive programmer from Henan University High School. He earned bronze medals at the 2009 and 2010 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). In 2011, he was admitted to Tsinghua University as a recommended student (baosong) following his achievements in the national informatics competitions.

noi_b_li_song_2009.gold: Li Song is a competitive programmer from Hainan Province. Representing Lingao Middle School, he won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) while in the ninth grade. He also achieved a first prize in the Senior Group of the 2010 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) in Hainan.

noi_b_yan_lifeng_2009.gold: Yan Lifeng (颜立峰) is a competitive programmer from Liuzhou High School in Guangxi. He won a bronze medal at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009) as a Grade 11 student. He also earned a first prize in the 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) while representing his high school.

noi_s_an_yujie_2009.gold: An Yujie is a competitive programmer from Daqing No. 1 Middle School. He earned a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and won three consecutive NOIP first prizes from 2007 to 2009. He later studied at the UM-SJTU Joint Institute of Shanghai Jiao Tong University, winning a first prize in the 2012 Mathematical Contest in Modeling.

noi_b_xin_chao_2009.gold: Xin Chao is a computer scientist from Shaoguan, Guangdong. A student at Guangdong Beijiang Middle School, he won bronze medals at the 2007 and 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He also earned a first prize in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) and later served as an informatics competition coach.

noi_b_zhu_xudong_2009.gold: Zhu Xudong (朱旭东) is a competitive programmer from Chongqing Bashu Secondary School. He won a bronze medal at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) with a score of 197. He was subsequently recommended for admission to Sun Yat-sen University, where he competed in the ACM-ICPC International Collegiate Programming Contest.

noi_b_fan_hongbo_2009.gold: Fan Hongbo (范鸿博) is a Chinese computer scientist and mathematician. A student at the No. 1 Middle School Affiliated to Central China Normal University, he won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He later pursued doctoral studies in mathematics at The Chinese University of Hong Kong, specializing in quasiconformal geometry and deep learning.
